description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who needs to prepare some messy survey data for model training.  
    A raw file already exists at  
        /home/user/data/raw_survey.txt  
    and your goal is to transform it – using only classic Unix text utilities (awk, sed, cut, sort, etc.) – into a clean, fully-specified training set plus a couple of helper artefacts.

    Step-by-step requirements
    1. Create the directory /home/user/data if it does not already exist.  
    2. Generate a cleaned CSV file at  
           /home/user/data/clean_survey.csv  
       following these exact rules:  
       • The file **must start with** the header line  
             id,name,age,country,income  
       • The raw file is pipe-delimited but has inconsistent spacing.  
       • Treat columns as: Name | Age | Country | Income (case insensitive).  
       • Discard every record whose Income field is literally “NA” (upper- or lower-case).  
       • Assign an integer id column starting at 1 and incrementing by 1 for each kept record, in the order they appear in the source file after cleaning.  
       • Trim all leading/trailing spaces from every field.  
       • Convert the Country value to upper-case (e.g., “usa” → “USA”).  
       • Write every record on its own line, no extra spaces, fields comma-separated, **no trailing comma**.  
       • Unix newline (\n) only – no Windows CR.

    3. Compute simple statistics from the cleaned CSV **using awk/sed/etc.** (no Python, no `bc`).  
       Produce a plain text file at  
           /home/user/data/income_stats.txt  
       whose contents are **exactly two lines** in this order:  
           average_income=INTEGER  
           median_income=INTEGER  
       • average_income is the arithmetic mean of the Income column, rounded down to the nearest whole integer (i.e., integer division discard remainder).  
       • median_income is the median of the Income column. If the list length is even, take the average of the two central values and again round down to an integer.  
       • Do not include thousands separators or any extra spaces.

    4. For automated grading, also write a mini log file to  
           /home/user/data/process.log  
       containing **precisely** the five lines below (newline-terminated), each in key=value form:  
           records_in_raw=<number of lines in raw file that actually contain data, excluding the header line>  
           records_with_income=<number of records that have a non-NA income and are therefore kept>  
           records_excluded=<number of records you discarded>  
           clean_file=/home/user/data/clean_survey.csv  
           stats_file=/home/user/data/income_stats.txt  

    5. You may create any temporary files you like, but they must live under /home/user and are not checked.

    Verification notes
    • The grader will open the three artefacts (clean_survey.csv, income_stats.txt, process.log) and compare them byte-for-byte to the expected results, so pay close attention to punctuation, ordering, capitalisation and newlines.  
    • Only use CLI tools available in a standard Debian/Ubuntu installation; assume no additional packages.  
    • You do **not** have root privileges.  
    • Carry out the whole task interactively in a Bash shell; do not hard-code file contents – derive them with awk/sed/etc.  

    Deliverables (must exist when you finish):
        /home/user/data/clean_survey.csv
        /home/user/data/income_stats.txt
        /home/user/data/process.log
truth:
Initial fixture supplied to the container before the agent starts:

    Path: /home/user/data/raw_survey.txt  
    Contents (including header line, LF line endings, no trailing blank line):
    ----------------------------------------
    Name | Age | Country | Income
    Alice   | 29 | usa | 70000
    Bob|34| Canada | 82000
       Charlie | NA | UK | NA
    Diana| 42|usa|90000
    Eve |27|Germany| 65000
    Frank | 31 | USA | 72000
    Grace| 25|  Canada|68000
    Heidi|38|UK | NA
    Ivan | 45 | germany| 87000
    Judy|30|USA| 75000
    ----------------------------------------

    Expected artefacts after the agent completes the task:

    1) /home/user/data/clean_survey.csv  
    ----------------------------------------
    id,name,age,country,income
    1,Alice,29,USA,70000
    2,Bob,34,CANADA,82000
    3,Diana,42,USA,90000
    4,Eve,27,GERMANY,65000
    5,Frank,31,USA,72000
    6,Grace,25,CANADA,68000
    7,Ivan,45,GERMANY,87000
    8,Judy,30,USA,75000
    ----------------------------------------

    2) /home/user/data/income_stats.txt  
    (exactly two lines, trailing newline on the second line only)
    ----------------------------------------
    average_income=76125
    median_income=73500
    ----------------------------------------

    3) /home/user/data/process.log  
    ----------------------------------------
    records_in_raw=10
    records_with_income=8
    records_excluded=2
    clean_file=/home/user/data/clean_survey.csv
    stats_file=/home/user/data/income_stats.txt
    ----------------------------------------
